title: Fix Stored XSS in Dictionary Definition Field
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — High Severity (CVSS 7.1)**

The Definition field in the Dictionary section was vulnerable to Stored XSS. Users with edit access could inject JavaScript payloads that would execute for all users visiting the Dictionary page.

## Fix

Input sanitization applied using an HTML purifier package to clean user input before storage, converting malicious characters to their HTML entity equivalents while preserving legitimate content.
acceptance_criteria: HTML and script tags are sanitized from the Definition field before storage
XSS payloads in the Definition field are neutralized
Legitimate text and formatting in Definition is preserved
Fix deployed to staging for pen test re-validation
middleware_changes: Input sanitization via HTML purifier package applied to the Definition field update controller.
implementation_notes: Used an HTML purifier package for more robust sanitization than strip_tags(), allowing safe HTML while removing malicious scripts. Deployed to staging.
comment_summary: Developer used a purifier package to sanitize input and pushed fix to staging.
